The ICLR'26 reviewer-name leak is a reminder that we're no longer advancing science -- we're trapped in a system that often disrespects it.

Maybe it's time for AI/ML to pause and rethink the entire conference culture.

Wild idea: Stop all major AI/ML conferences for 1-2 years. Let papers live on arXiv, and be submitted to journals. Meanwhile, conferences rebuild real policies and accountability.

Will this ever happen? Probably not.
The incentives are too big. Its about MONEY and PROFIT.

But the community deserves better and we need the courage to imagine alternatives.

ICLR has placed OpenReview in a difficult position, so I want to offer a few words about the OpenReview team working behind the scenes.

OpenReview has long been operated at UMass Amherst as a non-profit organization founded by Andrew McCallum. Each year, Andrew must raise more than $2 million to support a 20-person team that provides essential infrastructure for most major conferences.

I once asked Andrew what might have been a naïve question: whether he had considered developing a business model for OpenReview, given its prominence and the seemingly obvious opportunities. He pushed back, explaining that everything he has done for OpenReview is driven by a commitment to serve and strengthen the academic community. He is willing to devote significant personal effort to ensure the platform remains freely accessible to all.

We should not blame such a brilliant and dedicated team for an accidental issue. Otherwise, fewer people would be willing to shoulder this kind of responsibility in the future. 

Deep respect to the OpenReview team! I’m grateful for their work and happy to support in any way!

AI 顶会 ICLR 审稿人信息大规模泄露,官方连夜修 bug 也没挡住吃瓜群众 11 月 27 日,人工智能领域三大顶会之一的 2026 ICLR 审稿信息遭到泄露。会议方使用的 OpenReview 平台出现前端 bug,导致本应是双盲的审稿制度失效,只需要在浏览器上输入网址并替换某些字段即可查看审稿人身份和他的评审打分。该漏洞被发现后立即在 AI 社区内引发轩然大波。由于 OpenReview 平台是 AI 领域内使用广泛的会议论文审稿系统之一,很快有人发现这一漏洞不仅覆盖了新一届的 ICLR,还可令人自由查看往年甚至其他 AI 顶会(NeurIPS、ICML、ACL)的审稿信息。 有人发现给自己投稿论文打低分的审稿人是微信好友(ICLR 评审是 5 分制)。来源:社交媒体 几个小时后, ICLR 和 OpenReview 紧急修复了该漏洞,并发布声明,表示任何使用、暴露或分享泄露信息的人都会被拒稿且被 ICLR 长期列入黑名单,未来还将采取进一步行动处理此事。不过,已经有社交媒体用户称,有人爬走了整份审稿名单,还有人利用泄露数据做了统计分析。例如有帖子统计 ICLR 2026 前 1 万篇投稿的评审结果,结合审稿人的国别(主要语言)给出了平均打分习惯,发现韩国审稿人更习惯给低分;还有帖子称,基于同一份数据发现来自中国的审稿人占比高达 65.4%。目前,一些学者开始呼吁有关社区不要继续传播这份泄露名单,保护同行评审制度不被伤害。(ICLR, 机器之心)
ICLR 评审信息因系统漏洞泄露,官方紧急修复并警告违规者 北京时间 11 月 27 日晚,人工智能顶会 ICLR 的审稿系统 OpenReview 出现前端漏洞,导致审稿人身份及评分外泄。用户只需修改网址即可查看任意论文的审稿信息,使长期执行的双盲评审失效。 事件引发学术圈广泛关注,不少研究者发现低分评审... https://t.co/2jo4M1b5PY
